evals.expected_output: Asks dev vs production, collects client_id and client_secret separately, calls OAuth token endpoint, writes token and CYODA_ENV=development to .cyoda/config, confirms success without production warning

assertions.text: Asks whether this is development or production environment
Collects client_id and client_secret separately, one at a time
Does NOT echo client_secret back in the conversation
Does NOT show production warning for development login

evals.prompt: /cyoda:login — production environment, I accept the risk
evals.expected_output: Shows security warning, requires explicit 'yes', collects credentials, writes token and CYODA_ENV=production, shows prominent production reminder

assertions.text: Shows security warning about storing production credentials on disk
Requires explicit 'yes' — not any other response
Shows prominent production reminder after successful login

evals.prompt: /cyoda:login — production, but I'm not sure about storing credentials
evals.expected_output: Shows security warning, stops when user does not answer 'yes', does not write any credentials, suggests alternatives

assertions.text: Stops when user response is not explicit 'yes'
Does NOT write any credentials after a non-yes response
Suggests alternatives such as env vars or cyoda CLI
